notes: As of March 2, 2026 per DOJ Office of Pardon Attorney. NBC News analysis found $1.3B+ in restitution/fines forgiven across first 88 analyzed pardons. Standard lobbyist fees reportedly $1M; some sought $6M success fees.

pardons.name: Juan Orlando Hernández
pardons.offense: Drug trafficking conspiracy, weapons charges — as President of Honduras (2014–2022) accepted millions in bribes from drug cartels including the Sinaloa cartel, facilitated shipment of 400+ tons of cocaine to the United States
pardons.original_sentence: 45 years imprisonment (sentenced 2024 after extradition and jury trial in New York)

financial_connection.description: No direct Trump financial connection identified

political_connection.description: Trump announced the pardon while publicly endorsing Nasry 'Tito' Asfura — candidate of Hernández's National Party — in Honduran presidential elections. Framed as rewarding a pro-US ally; critics say it undermines US drug enforcement credibility. Honduras Attorney General and Interpol sought Hernández's arrest after the US pardon. Congressional resolutions introduced condemning the pardon (H.Res. 929, S.Res. 530).

sources.title: Trump formally pardons former Honduran President Juan Orlando Hernández
Examining Trump's Pardon of Former Honduran President Convicted of Trafficking Drugs to U.S.
Juan Orlando Hernández Pardon: Implications for U.S. Foreign Policy

pardons.notes: Most geopolitically significant pardon of second term. Hernández was convicted of helping cartels ship 400+ tons of cocaine to the US while president. Trump pardoned him while endorsing his party's candidate in Honduran elections — a direct link between clemency and foreign electoral influence. Honduras issued an Interpol red notice seeking re-arrest. Congressional bipartisan condemnation (H.Res. 929, S.Res. 530).
